# Getting Started with Managed Omni Gateway on CloudHub 2.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Get started with Anypoint Managed Omni Gateway on CloudHub 2.0. Create and deploy a Managed Omni
Gateway, publish an API, and secure the API with a basic authentication policy.

## Before You Begin

Before getting started with Omni Gateway, complete these steps:

- Review the [Requirements and Limits for Omni Gateway](flex-review-prerequisites) and verify that
  you have the necessary Managed Omni Gateway permissions.
- Create a private space in CloudHub 2.0 or verify that you can access an existing private space. To
  create a private space, see [Creating Private Spaces](../../cloudhub-2/ps-create-configure).

## Deploy a Managed Omni Gateway

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Click **Omni Gateways** in the sidebar.
3.  Click **Managed Omni Gateway**.
4.  Click **Add Managed Omni Gateway**.
5.  Enter a **Gateway name**.
6.  Select a your private space as your **Deployment target**.
7.  Click **Advanced options**.
8.  Copy and save the Managed Omni Gateway public endpoint for later testing. This is the HTTP or
    HTTPs URL that appears in the **Public endpoint** UI block, for example,
    `[https://my-gateway-abc123.edf456.us-east1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/](https://my-gateway-abc123.edf456.us-east1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/)`.
9.  Click **Save & deploy**.

    The UI returns to the dashboard of your new Managed Omni Gateway.

10. Wait until your gatewayâs **Status** is **Running**.

## Publish a Simple API

1.  From your Managed Gatewayâs dashboard, click **Add API instance**.
2.  From the **Add API** page, select **Omni Gateway** as your runtime.
3.  Select your deployed Managed Omni Gateway in the **Select a gateway** section.
4.  Click **Next**.
5.  Click **Create New API**.
6.  Specify an API name and select **HTTP API** as the asset type.
7.  Click **Next**.
8.  Donât configure any downstream options.
9.  Click **Next**.
10. From the **Upstream** page:
    1.  Enter this URL in the **Upstream URL** field:

        ```none
        https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/
        ```

11. Click **Next**.
12. Click **Save & Deploy**.

    The UI takes you to the **API Summary** page of the newly created API instance.

    All HTTP requests made to the public endpoint are now proxied to the `jsonplaceholder` service.

13. To test the API instance, replace the public endpoint placeholder and run this command in a new
    terminal window:

    ```ssh
    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" --request GET '<publicEndpoint>'
    ```

    The command executes a `GET` request to the API, and then prints the resulting `200` status
    code, indicating success.

## Secure Your API Using the Basic Authentication Policy

1.  From the **API Summary** page, select **Policies** from the side navigation panel.
2.  In the **Policies** page, click **Add inbound policy**.
3.  Select the **Basic Authentication - Simple** policy.
4.  Click **Next**.
5.  For **User Name**, enter `user`.
6.  For **User Password**, enter `password`.
7.  Click **Apply**.
8.  To test the API Instance _without_ authentication, replace the public endpoint placeholder and
    run this command in the terminal window:

    ```ssh
    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" --request GET '<publicEndpoint>'
    ```

    The command prints a resulting `401 (Unauthorized)` status code, because the request does not
    include the Authentication context.

9.  To test the API Instance _with_ authentication, replace the public endpoint placeholder and run
    this command in the terminal window:

    ```ssh
    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" --request GET '<publicEndpoint>' -u user:password
    ```

    > [!NOTE] The `user` and `password` parameters must match what was specified when you applied
    > the policy via **API Manager**.

    The command prints the resulting `200` status code, indicating success.

## Next Steps

- [Requirements and Limits for Omni Gateway](flex-review-prerequisites)
- [Setting Up Self-Managed Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-set-up)
---

# Deploying Agent Network Ingress and Egress Managed Omni Gateways

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Agent Network deployments require both an ingress and egress Omni Gateway. The ingress Omni Gateway
sits in front of broker and API endpoints to manage and enforce policies on traffic entering the
network. The egress Omni Gateway sits on outbound paths from brokers and agents to external agents
and enforces policies, manages connections, and emits telemetry data.

The only functional difference between ingress and egress Omni Gateways, is that the egress Gateway
doesnât have a public endpoint. Only clients inside the same private space as the egress gateway can
make requests to it. Traffic leaving the private space to external services passes through the
egress gateway.

![Agent Network showing agents and MCP servers defined in YAML and published to Exchange, with traffic through Omni Gateway, governed by API Manager, and observed in Monitoring and Visualizer](_images/agent-fabric-architecture.png)

> [!NOTE] For a more detailed description of this architecture diagram, see
> [Agent Network Architecture](../../anypoint-code-builder/af-agent-networks#agent-network-architecture).

## Deploy Ingress and Egress Managed Omni Gateways

The easiest way to deploy your Agent Network ingress and egress Omni Gateways is by using Anypoint
Code Builder or Anypoint Command Line Interface (CLI):

- [Set Up Agent Network Gateways by Using Anypoint Code Builder](../../anypoint-code-builder/af-get-started#set-up-agent-network-gateways-for-your-target-space)
- [Set Up Agent Network Gateways by Using Anypoint CLI](../../anypoint-cli/latest/agent-fabric#agent-network-setup-gateways)

To manually deploy both ingress and egress managed Omni Gateways using the Runtime Manager UI, see
[Deploy a Managed Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-managed-set-up).

For ingress gateways, configure a **Public endpoint** in the advanced options **Ingress** tab. For
egress gateways, verify that thereâs no **Public endpoint** configured. To prevent confusion, give a
descriptive name defining the gateway as either ingress or egress.

## Apply Inbound and Outbound Policies

Inbound policies are policies that are enforced on all traffic accessing an instanceâs endpoint.
Outbound policies are policies that are enforced on all traffic accessing a specific URL and are
only applied to that URL. For example, for an agent instance that has multiple upstreams, an inbound
client ID enforcement policy enforces client ID verification for all traffic accessing the agent
instance. However, an outbound policy might be applied to only one of the instanceâs upstream.

## Next Steps

- [Requirements and Limits for Omni Gateway](flex-review-prerequisites)
- [Setting Up Self-Managed Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-set-up)
---

# Deploy a Managed Omni Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Deploy a Managed Omni Gateway to CloudHub 2.0 or a Runtime Fabric namespace to launch a completely
managed gateway.

## Before You Begin

### CloudHub 2.0

Before getting started with Omni Gateway on CloudHub 2.0, make sure:

- The business group you want to deploy your gateway to has Managed Omni Gateway Resources. To
  redestribute Managed Omni Gateway resources to a business group, see
  [Redistribute Resources Between Existing Business Groups](../../access-management/business-groups#redistribute-resources).
- [Create a CloudHub 2.0 Private Space](../../cloudhub-2/ps-create-configure)

### Runtime Fabric

Before getting started with Omni Gateway on Runtime Fabric, make sure:

- Your Runtime Fabric deployment version is 2.11.0 or later. To upgrade, see
  [Upgrading Runtime Fabric](../../runtime-fabric/latest/upgrade-index).
- The business group you want to deploy your gateway to has Managed Omni Gateway Resources. To
  redestribute Managed Omni Gateway resources to a business group, see
  [Redistribute Resources Between Existing Business Groups](../../access-management/business-groups#redistribute-resources).

  > [!NOTE] Gateways cannot be shared across business groups. A user must be a member of the
  > business group to access the Omni Gateway.

- Run this command in the same namespace as your Runtime Fabric agent to enable the
  `ManagedFlexGateway` resource:

  ```bash
  # When performing the helm upgrade for the first time, during Runtime Fabric creation:
  helm upgrade runtime-fabric rtf/rtf-agent -n <rtf-agent-namespace> --version <target-rtf-version> --set global.crds.flex.enabled=true

  # When enabling Managed Flex Gateway for an existing runtime fabric:
  helm upgrade runtime-fabric rtf/rtf-agent -n <rtf-agent-namespace> --reset-then-reuse-values --set global.crds.flex.enabled=true
  ```

> [!NOTE] For Omni Gateway Agent Fabric deployments on Runtime Fabric, you must enable Open
> Telemetry with a `100` percent **Sampling percentage**. You canât override the sampling percentage
> for individual instances.

## Deploy a Managed Omni Gateway

1.  Log in to [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/login/) with your Anypoint username
    and password.
2.  Select **Runtime Manager**.
3.  Ensure youâre in environment where you would like to deploy your Managed Gateway
4.  Click **Omni Gateways** in the side navigation panel.
5.  Click **Managed Omni Gateway**.
6.  Click **Add Managed Omni Gateway**.
7.  Enter a **Gateway Name**.
8.  Select the **Deployment Target** where you want to deploy your Managed Omni Gateway.
9.  Select a **Release Channel**:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Release channel</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Edge</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This channel releases a new minor version&nbsp;every four months. It has the latest features and shorter support windows.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Long-Term Support</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This channel releases a new minor version&nbsp;once a year.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    To learn more about the differences between release channels, see
    [Omni Gateway Version Lifecycle](flex-gateway-version-lifecycle).

10. Select **Version**.

    Select the latest version unless if a later version is specifically needed.

11. Select **Gateway type**:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Small Managed Omni Gateway</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use for testing and staging purposes. Small Managed Omni Gateways can deploy up to 50 APIs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Large Managed Omni Gateway</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use for production purposes. Large Managed Omni Gateways can deploy up to 500 APIs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Extra Large Managed Omni Gateway</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use for production purposes with higher throughput and more process demanding policies. Extra Large Managed Omni Gateways can deploy up to 500 APIs.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    To learn more about Managed Omni Gateway sizing, see
    [Managed Omni Gateway Limits](flex-review-prerequisites#managed-flex-gateway-limits).

12. Configure **Advanced options**:

    Ingress

    You canât configure the ingress options for a Managed Omni Gateway deployed to a CloudHub 2.0
    shared space. Shared Spaces donât support vanity domains.
    - **Public endpoint**:
      1.  Select your desired private space **Domain**.

          To learn more, see [Creating Private Spaces](../../cloudhub-2/ps-create-configure).

      2.  Enter a **Host**.
      3.  Click **Add Endpoint** to add an additional endpoint to a different domain.

          Managed Omni Gateway supports one **Public endpoint** per domain in your private space.

    - **Ingress options**:
      - **Forward SSL Session**:

        Enables SSL forwarding during a session.

        SSL forwarding is mostly used with client authentication. See
        [Enable Client Authentication](../../anypoint-security/enable-client-authentication). SSL
        forwarding forwards client certificate details in HTTP request headers so they are available
        to the gateway. These fields can identify an authenticated client and allow the gateway
        policies to determine and use the identity.

        The following headers are available:

        <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Header Name</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>x-ssl-client-verify</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SUCCESS/FAIL</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>x-ssl-issuer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client certificate issuer</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>x-ssl-client-serial</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client certificate serial number</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>x-ssl-client-dn</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contents of the client certificate DN field</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>x-ssl-client-cert</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Contents of the client certificate</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

      - **Last-mile Security**:

        Specifies that TLS termination and decryption for the forwarded HTTPS connections occurs in
        the gateway.

    Properties

    To configure how long Managed Omni Gateway waits for response or idle connections, configure the
    timeout properties:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Parameter</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Description</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Upstream Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time a upstream connection can remain idle without receiving client requests. Upstream responses are not included in this timeout.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Upstream Response Timeout</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time the upstream service has to respond from when Omni Gateway sends a request.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Stream Idle Timeout</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time a stream can remain idle without receiving additional client requests or upstream responses.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    You can also update timeout values later from **Runtime Configurations** in Runtime Manager. For
    details, see [Configure Timeouts for Managed Omni Gateway](flex-managed-timeout).

    Logging
    - **Forward application logs to Anypoint Monitoring**:

      Forwards Omni Gateway logs to Anypoint Monitoring. When disabled, logs are only available in
      Runtime Manager.

    - **Additional Log Levels**:
      - **INFO**: Informative messages
      - **DEBUG**: Debugging messages
      - **WARNING**: Warning messages
      - **ERROR**: Error messages, such as when an exception occurs
      - **FATAL**: Fatal messages for when an application fails

        > [!NOTE] You can use Access Management audit logs to track Managed Omni Gateway actions,
        > such as creating, editing, starting, and stopping gateways. Audit logs don't require
        > configuration.
        >
        > To learn more, see xref:flex-managed-view-status.adoc#audit-logs\[View Managed Omni
        > Gateway Audit Logs\].

    Distributed Tracing
    - **Distributed tracing**:

      Enable to generate OpenTelemetry distributed tracing data for the Managed Omni Gateway. When
      enabled, you can set the sampling percentage.

    - **Sampling percentage**:

      Set the overall distributed tracing sampling percentage. This setting corresponds to the
      `overall` sampling percentage. The `client` and `random` sampling percentages are set to 100%
      by default, if the sampling percentage is set to a non-zero value here.

      Set the **Sampling percentage** to 0 if you want to specify the sampling percentage only on a
      per-API basis, using the Tracing policy.

      > [!NOTE] For Omni Gateway Agent Fabric deployments on Runtime Fabric, you must enable Open
      > Telemetry with a `100` percent **Sampling percentage**. You cannot override the sampling
      > percentage for individual instances.

13. Click **Save & deploy**.

## Managed Omni Gateway Endpoints

Omni Gateway provides these endpoints:

- **Public Endpoint**: The public endpoint is exposed to the public internet. Traffic comes from the
  internet through the CloudHub 2.0 load balancer.
- **Internal Endpoint**: The internal endpoint is not publicly exposed on the internet, but traffic
  still goes through the CloudHub 2.0 load balancer for internal traffic within the private space.
  Use an internal endpoint when you donât know if traffic comes from inside the cluster or from a
  connected network. This is only available for Managed Omni Gateways deployed to a CloudHub 2.0
  Private Space.
- **Cluster Endpoint**: Only accessible from inside the cluster. Use when the gateway is acting as
  an egress gateway receiving traffic from internal apps. Port 8082 is reserved for cluster endpoint
  traffic.

To find the endpoint names:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager > Omni Gateways**.
2.  Click the Managed Omni Gateway name.
3.  Copy your endpoint from the dashboard.

## Edit a Managed Omni Gateway Settings

To edit a Managed Omni Gateway setting:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager > Omni Gateways**.
2.  Click the Managed Omni Gateway name.
3.  In the navigation menu, click Settings.
4.  Make the necessary edits.
5.  Click **Apply changes**.

## Remove a Public Endpoint

To remove an endpoint:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager > Omni Gateways**.
2.  Click the Managed Omni Gateway name.
3.  In the navigation menu, click Settings.
4.  Click **Advanced options**.
5.  In the **Public endpoint** row, click **X** (Delete).
6.  Click **Apply changes**.

## Next Steps

- [Requirements and Limits for Omni Gateway](flex-review-prerequisites)
- [Setting Up Self-Managed Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-set-up)
---

# Upgrade or Delete Managed Omni Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can upgrade or delete a Managed Omni Gateway using Anypoint Runtime Manager.

Managed Omni Gateways are regularly auto-upgraded, depending on your Gateway release channel (LTS or
Edge). See [Omni Gateway Version Lifecycle](flex-gateway-version-lifecycle) for more information.

You can upgrade a Managed Omni Gateway earlier than the regularly scheduled upgrade or patching
process, between the new release date and the auto-upgrade date. You can choose the time that better
suits the needs of your organization.

## Early Upgrade Managed Omni Gateway (Optional)

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Omni Gateways** in the navigation menu.
3.  Select the **Managed Omni Gateway** tab.
4.  Click the name of the gateway to upgrade.
5.  Click **Settings** in the navigation menu.
6.  If a newer version is available, the **Version** field shows an **Update available** tag.
7.  Click the padlock symbol next to the version and **Update available** tag.
8.  Click the **Apply Changes** button.  
    A status message (**Your gateway is being updated**) appears while the upgrade is being applied.
    A success message (**Your gateway has been successfully updated**) appears when the upgrade is
    complete.
9.  Click **Dashboard** in the navigation menu to view your gateway status.

## Delete Managed Omni Gateway

You can delete a Managed Omni Gateway using Anypoint Runtime Manager.

### Before You Begin

If there are any APIs associated with the gateway you want to delete, you must either move the APIs
to another gateway or delete the APIs. After any associated APIs are moved or deleted, follow these
steps to delete a gateway and confirm the deletion.

### Delete Gateway

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Omni Gateways** in the navigation menu.
3.  Select the **Managed Omni Gateway** tab.
4.  Click the name of the gateway to delete.
5.  Ensure the action menu button says **Delete Gateway**. If it says **Stop Gateway**, click the
    down arrow part of this button, and select **Delete Gateway** to change the action.
6.  Click the action menu button **Delete Gateway**.
7.  Verify that you delete the correct gateway in the confirmation dialog.
8.  Click **Yes, Delete**.  
    A status message (**Your gateway is being deleted**) appears while the action is applied. A
    success message (**Your gateway has been successfully deleted**) appears when the action is
    complete.

> [!NOTE] If you delete a gateway, you lose its configuration history.

Mode) or declarative configuration files (Local Mode).

# Omni Gateway Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Omni Gateway is an Envoy-based, ultrafast lightweight API gateway designed to manage and secure APIs
running anywhere. Built to seamlessly integrate with DevOps and CI/CD workflows, Omni Gateway
delivers the performance required for the most demanding applications and microservices while
providing enterprise security and manageability across any environment.

The following release notes are provided for Omni Gateway:

- [Omni Gateway Release Notes](../../release-notes/flex-gateway/flex-gateway-release-notes)

# Renew Self-Managed Omni Gateway Registration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Omni Gateway uses a managed PKI certificate to communicate with Anypoint Platform. This certificate
is generated when you register Omni Gateway in either Connected Mode or Local Mode. Updating the
certificate ensures the continued operation of your applications.

You update a certificate by renewing registration via the `flexctl registration renew` command.

- The `renew` command renews an existing Omni Gateway registration with Runtime Manager.
- For authentication, you run the `renew` command with either user credentials or with connected app
  credentials. Authentication with user credentials requires the `--username` and `--password`
  flags. Authentication with connected app credentials requires the `--client-id` and
  `--client-secret` flags.

You verify the renewal process via the `flexctl registration inspect` command.

- The `inspect` command extracts registration information you can use to verify whether the `renew`
  command was successful.
- You can also use the `inspect` command to first determine if renewing registration is actually
  required. The `inspect` command returns the certificate expiration date.
- You can verify (inspect) renewal by using the default method, or you can specify your existing
  registration file. If you run the `inspect` command without flags, the command inspects
  registration status by using the default method.

To avoid disruptions:

1.  [Download the latest version of Omni Gateway.](#before-you-begin)
2.  Renew your Omni Gateway registration.

    Choose one of two methods:
    - [Anypoint Platform User Credentials](#renew-with-user-password)
    - [Connected App Credentials](#renew-with-conn-app)

3.  Verify the success of the renewal process.

    Choose one of two methods:
    - [Default Method](#inspect-with-custom-url)
    - [Registration File](#inspect-with-reg-file)

## Before You Begin

To invoke the registration renewal `flexctl` command, download the latest version of Omni Gateway.
You donât need to register and run this version of the gateway.

- [Downloading Omni Gateway](flex-install)

## Renew Registration with Anypoint Platform User Credentials

1.  Create a new directory called `flex-renew-registration` (or similar). You run the `renew`
    command in this new directory. The command creates a new registration file.

    > [!NOTE] Registration renewal fails if the `renew` command is run in a directory containing an
    > existing registration file.

2.  Use the following command:
    - Linux
    - Docker
    - Podman

    ```ssh
    flexctl registration renew --username=<your-username> --password=<your-password> \
     <path-to-registration-file>
    ```

    Replace `<your-username>` and `<your-password>` with Anypoint Platform user credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file.

    Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

    For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `renew` as part of the container `run` command.

    ```ssh
    docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
    -v "$(pwd)":/renew \
    -v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration \
    -u $UID mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --username=<your-username> \
    --password=<your-password> \
    --output-directory=/renew \
    <path-to-registration-file-in-container>
    ```

    Replace `<your-username>` and `<your-password>` with Anypoint Platform user credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file-in-container>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file. You must specify the file in the container file system, which in this
    case is `/registration/registration.yaml`.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

    As an example, if `/home/user/flex/registration.yaml` contains your current registration file,
    navigate to `/home/user/flex/` and run the following command with your Anypoint Platform
    credentials:

    ```ssh
    docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
    -u $UID -v "$(pwd)":/registration  \
    mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --username=<your-username> \
    --password=<your-password> \
    --output-directory=/registration/new \
    /registration/registration.yaml
    ```

    The command creates a new registration file: `/home/user/flex/new/registration.yaml`.

    For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `renew` as part of the container `run` command.

    ```ssh
    podman run --entrypoint flexctl --userns=keep-id \
    -v "$(pwd)":/renew:Z \
    -v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration:Z \
    -u $UID docker.io/mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --username=<your-username> \
    --password=<your-password> \
    --output-directory=/renew \
    <path-to-registration-file-in-container>
    ```

    Replace `<your-username>` and `<your-password>` with Anypoint Platform user credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file-in-container>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file. You must specify the file in the container file system, which in this
    case is `/registration/registration.yaml`.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

    > [!NOTE] Disable MFA for your Anypoint Platform account prior to running the `renew` command
    > with user credentials.

3.  Run the command.

    The output should include the message:

    ```text
    Registration renewal completed
    ```

4.  Copy the newly created registration file to the runtime registration file location. For example:

    `/usr/local/share/mulesoft/flex-gateway/conf.d/registration.yaml`

5.  Restart the Omni Gateway replica if one is already running.

## Renew Registration with Connected App Credentials

1.  If you donât have a username or password for Anypoint Platform, you can
    [configure a connected app](../../access-management/connected-apps-developers#create-a-connected-app)
    via Anypoint Access Management.
    1.  Include the following scopes:
        - Read Servers
        - Manage Servers
        - View Organization

    2.  Save the **Id** and **Secret** of the connected app you configure, then use these
        credentials in the `renew` command.

2.  Create a new directory called `flex-renew-registration` (or similar). You run the `renew`
    command in this new directory. The command creates a new registration file.

    > [!NOTE] Registration renewal fails if the `renew` command is run in a directory containing an
    > existing registration file.

3.  Use the following command:
    - Linux
    - Docker
    - Podman

    ```ssh
    flexctl registration renew --client-id=<your-client-id> --client-secret=<your-client-secret> \
     <path-to-registration-file>
    ```

    Replace `<your-client-id>` and `<your-client-secret>` with the connected app credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file.

    Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

    For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `renew` as part of the container `run` command.

    ```ssh
    docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
    -v "$(pwd)":/renew \
    -v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration \
    -u $UID mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --client-id=<your-client-id> \
    --client-secret=<your-client-secret> \
    --output-directory=/renew \
    <path-to-registration-file-in-container>
    ```

    Replace `<your-client-id>` and `<your-client-secret>` with the connected app credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file-in-container>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file. You must specify the file in the container file system, which in this
    case is `/registration/registration.yaml`.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

    As an example, if `/home/user/flex/registration.yaml` contains your current registration file,
    navigate to `/home/user/flex/` and run the following command with your connected app
    credentials:

    ```ssh
    docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
    -u $UID -v "$(pwd)":/registration  \
    mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --client-id=<your-client-id> \
    --client-secret=<your-client-secret> \
    --output-directory=/registration/new \
    /registration/registration.yaml
    ```

    The command creates a new registration file: `/home/user/flex/new/registration.yaml`.

    For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `renew` as part of the container `run` command.

    ```ssh
    podman run --entrypoint flexctl --userns=keep-id \
    -v "$(pwd)":/renew:Z \
    -v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration:Z \
    -u $UID docker.io/mulesoft/flex-gateway \
    registration renew \
    --client-id=<your-client-id> \
    --client-secret=<your-client-secret> \
    --output-directory=/renew \
    <path-to-registration-file-in-container>
    ```

    Replace `<your-client-id>` and `<your-client-secret>` with the connected app credentials.

    Replace `<path-to-registration-file-in-container>` with the path and filename of the existing
    `registration.yaml` file. You must specify the file in the container file system, which in this
    case is `/registration/registration.yaml`.

    To update environment URLs in your registration file, such as the metering environment URL, use
    the `--update-urls=true` flag.

4.  Run the command.

    The output should include the message:

    ```text
    Registration renewal completed
    ```

5.  Copy the newly created registration file to the runtime registration file location. For example:

    `/usr/local/share/mulesoft/flex-gateway/conf.d/registration.yaml`

6.  Restart the Omni Gateway replica if one is already running.

## Verify Registration Status with the Default Method

To inspect a registration, run the following command:

- Linux
- Docker
- Podman

```ssh
flexctl registration inspect
```

Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

For Omni Gateway running in a container, run `inspect` as part of the container `exec` command.
Replace `<container-name-or-id>` with the running container reference.

```ssh
docker exec -u $UID <container-name-or-id> flexctl registration inspect
```

For Omni Gateway running in a container, run `inspect` as part of the container `exec` command.
Replace `<container-name-or-id>` with the running container reference.

```ssh
podman exec -u $UID <container-name-or-id> flexctl registration inspect
```

The output should include the date of certificate expiration. For example:

```text
{âexpiration_dateâ: â2025-09-25 19:27:32 +0000 UTCâ}
```

## Verify Registration Status with a Registration File

You can inspect a registration by extracting the information from your `registration.yaml` file.
Specify the path and filename.

To inspect a registration, run the following command:

- Linux
- Docker
- Podman

```ssh
flexctl registration inspect --file=<path-to-registration-file>
```

Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `inspect` as part of the container `run` command.

```ssh
docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
-v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration \
-u $UID mulesoft/flex-gateway \
registration inspect --file=<path-to-registration-file>
```

For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `inspect` as part of the container `run` command.

```ssh
podman run --entrypoint flexctl --userns=keep-id \
-v <path-to-registration-directory>:/registration:Z \
-u $UID docker.io/mulesoft/flex-gateway \
registration inspect --file=<path-to-registration-file>
```

The output should include the date of certificate expiration:

```text
{âexpiration_dateâ: â2025-09-25 19:27:32 +0000 UTCâ}
```

As an example, if your current directory contains the registration file, run the following command:

```ssh
docker run --entrypoint flexctl \
-v "$(pwd)":/registration \
-u $UID mulesoft/flex-gateway \
registration inspect --file=/registration/registration.yaml
```

## Renew Registration for Omni Gateway Running in Kubernetes

The following example describes a scenario where Omni Gateway is installed in a `gateway` namespace
with an `ingress` release name, and where registration is stored in the Kubernetes database.

1.  Recover the `registration.yaml` file with the following `kubectl` command:

    ```ssh
    kubectl -n gateway get secret "$(kubectl -n gateway get deployment ingress -o=jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.volumes[?(@.name=="registration")].secret.secretName}')" -o=jsonpath='{.data.registration\.yaml}' | base64 --decode > registration.yaml
    ```

    The command stores the registration file in `./registration.yaml`.

2.  Run the `flexctl registration renew` command, as described for Docker in previous sections:
    - [Renew with Anypoint Platform User Credentials](#renew-with-user-password)
    - [Renew with Connected App Credentials](#renew-with-conn-app)

3.  Upgrade Omni Gateway with the following `helm` command:

    ```ssh
    helm get values -n gateway ingress > values.yaml \
    && helm upgrade -n gateway ingress flex-gateway/flex-gateway --wait -f values.yaml --set-file registration.content=new/registration.yaml
    ```

## Get Help with the Renewal and Verification Commands

You can run the following `help` commands for information about usage:

- Renewal
  - Linux
  - Docker
  - Podman

  ```ssh
  flexctl registration renew --help
  ```

  Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

  For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `help` as part of the container `run` command.

  ```ssh
  docker run --entrypoint flexctl mulesoft/flex-gateway registration renew --help
  ```

  For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `help` as part of the container `run` command.

  ```ssh
  podman run --entrypoint flexctl docker.io/mulesoft/flex-gateway registration renew --help
  ```

- Verification
  - Linux
  - Docker
  - Podman

  ```ssh
  flexctl registration inspect --help
  ```

  Use `sudo` if you encounter file permission issues when running this command.

  For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `help` as part of the container `run` command.

  ```ssh
  docker run --entrypoint flexctl mulesoft/flex-gateway registration inspect --help
  ```

  For Omni Gateway running in a container, invoke `help` as part of the container `run` command.

  ```ssh
  podman run --entrypoint flexctl docker.io/mulesoft/flex-gateway registration inspect --help
  ```

## Renew an Expired Registration

Attempting to run Omni Gateway with an expired certificate in Connected Mode results in the
following:

- New replicas fail to download API configurations from Anypoint Platform, thereby becoming
  unusable. Existing clusters fail to reload.
- Logs and metrics fail to upload to Anypoint Platform. Troubleshooting using Anypoint Platform is
  not possible.
- Metering information fails to upload to Anypoint Platform. MuleSoft is unable to collect or report
  usage metrics.

Attempting to run Omni Gateway with an expired certificate in Local Mode results in the following:

- Metering information fails to upload to Anypoint Platform. MuleSoft is unable to collect or report
  usage metrics.

If a certificate expires, you can still invoke `flexctl registration renew`, which updates the
expired certificate.

## See Also

- [Downloading Omni Gateway](flex-install)
- [Registering and Running Omni Gateway in Connected Mode](flex-conn-reg-run)
- [Registering and Running Omni Gateway in Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run)

# Securing Omni Gateway Instances with Policies

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Policies enforce rules when Omni Gateway processes requests, enabling you to secure and govern your
Omni Gateway API, agent, and tool instances.

MuleSoft provides policies that you can apply to your instances via Anypoint Platform or declarative
configuration files. The provided policies include rate limiting, caching, authentication,
authorization, threat protection, monitoring, logging. For more information about MuleSoft-provided
policies, see the [Inbound Policies Directory](policies-included-directory) and
[Outbound Policies Directory](policies-outbound-directory).

To extend existing functionality or define new functionality, create custom policies based on your
specific business requirements. For information about creating custom policies, see
[Omni Gateway Policy Development Kit (PDK) Overview](../../pdk/latest/policies-pdk-overview).

Automated policies enable your instances to comply with common security and logging requirements by
automatically applying the same set of policies to all instances running in Omni Gateway. See
[Automated Policies](policies-automated-overview) for more information.

To apply a policy for Managed Omni Gateway or Connected Mode, see
[Applying Policies for Managed Omni Gateways and Connected Mode](flex-gateway-secure-conn).

To apply a policy in Local Mode, see [Applying Policies in Local Mode](flex-gateway-secure-local).

For information about policy reordering, see [Ordering Policies](policies-reorder).

For information about DataWeave support, see
[DataWeave Support in Omni Gateway Policies](policies-flex-dataweave-support).

## See Also

- [Omni Gateway Overview](./)
- [Getting Started with Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-getting-started-overview)
---

# Applying Policies for Managed Omni Gateways and Connected Mode

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

For Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway running in Connected Mode, you apply inbound and outbound
policies using **API Manager**. To find the policies provided by Omni Gateway, see
[Inbound Policies Directory](policies-included-directory) and
[Outbound Policies Directory](policies-outbound-directory).

## Apply Inbound Policies

1.  Go to **Anypoint Platform > API Manager**.
2.  From **API Instances** or **Agent and Tool Instances**, click the name of the instance to which
    you want to apply a policy.
3.  From the side navigation panel, click **Policies**.
4.  Click **\+ Add policy**.
5.  Select the policy to apply.
6.  Configure the required parameters.

    For policy configuration parameters, see
    [Inbound Policies Directory](policies-included-directory).

7.  Click **Advanced options** to configure these optional parameters:
    - **Policy label**: A label to identify the policy, specifically for use cases with multiples of
      the same policy.
    - **Policy version**: The version of the policy definition to use.
    - Application conditions: Apply the policy to all incoming requests or requests that match
      specified conditions:
      - **Methods**: The HTTP methods to apply the policy to.
      - **URI template regex**: A URI template regex of which request paths to apply the policy to.
      - **Host**: The host to apply the policy to.
      - **Headers**: A map of headers names and regex values to apply the policy to.

        > [!NOTE] If necessary, use multiples of the same policy to meet all required conditions.

8.  Click **Apply**.

## Apply Outbound Policies

1.  Go to **Anypoint Platform > API Manager**.
2.  From **API Instances** or **Agent and Tool Instances**, click the name of the instance to which
    you want to apply a policy.
3.  From the side navigation panel, click **Policies**.
4.  Click the **Outbound policies** tab.
5.  Click **\+ Add outbound policy**.
6.  Select the **Upstream** to which you want to apply a policy.
7.  If you want to apply the policy to an additional upstream, click **Add upstream** and select the
    additional upstream. Repeat this step for all upstreams to which you want to apply the policy.
8.  Click **Next**.
9.  Select the policy to apply.
10. Configure the required parameters.

    For policy configuration parameters, see
    [Outbound Policies Directory](policies-outbound-directory).

11. Click **Advanced options** to configure these optional parameters:
    - Policy label: A label to identify the policy, specifically for use cases with multiples of the
      same policy.
    - **Policy version**: The version of the policy definition to use.
    - Application conditions: Apply the policy to all incoming requests or requests that match
      specified conditions:
      - **Methods**: The HTTP methods to apply the policy to.
      - **Headers**: A map of headers names and regex values to apply the policy to.

        > [!NOTE] If necessary, use multiples of the same policy to meet all required conditions.

12. Click **Apply**.

## See Also

- [Omni Gateway Overview](./)
- [Getting Started with Self-Managed Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-getting-started)

# Uninstall Self-Managed Omni Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can uninstall Omni Gateway running as a Linux service, in a container, or in a Kubernetes
cluster.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#linux"><img src="_images/install-linux-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#docker"><img src="_images/install-docker-logo.png" alt="25%" width="25%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#kubernetes"><img src="_images/install-kubernetes-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#linux">Uninstall Omni Gateway Running as a Linux Service</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#docker">Uninstall Omni Gateway Running in a Docker Container</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#kubernetes">Uninstall Omni Gateway Running in a Kubernetes Cluster</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Uninstall Omni Gateway Running as a Linux Service

Omni Gateway can be uninstalled as any other package:

```ssh
sudo apt remove -y flex-gateway
```

## Uninstall Omni Gateway Running in a Docker Container

To uninstall Omni Gateway, stop and remove the Omni Gateway container:

1.  Determine the Omni Gateway container ID by listing all the running containers:

    ```ssh
    docker ps
    ```

2.  In the list of containers, note the container ID associated with the Omni Gateway container you
    want to uninstall.
3.  Stop the Omni Gateway container:

    ```ssh
    docker stop <container id>
    ```

    Replace `<container-id` with the container ID of the Omni Gateway container you want to stop:

4.  Remove the Omni Gateway container:

    ```ssh
    docker rm <container id>
    ```

    Replace `<container-id` with the container ID of the Omni Gateway container you want to remove.
    The command permanently deletes the container and its associated resources.

5.  Optionally, remove the Omni Gateway container image:

    ```ssh
    docker rmi mulesoft/flex-gateway
    ```

6.  To confirm that Omni Gateway is uninstalled, run the `docker ps` command again. The uninstalled
    container no longer appears in the list.

## Uninstall Omni Gateway Running in a Kubernetes Cluster

To uninstall Omni Gateway running in a Kubernetes cluster:

1.  Uninstall the Helm release. The following example removes a release called âmy-releaseâ:

    ```ssh
    helm delete my-release
    ```

    > [!NOTE] Update `my-release` to the release you have running. To see a list of all releases,
    > use the command `helm list -A`.

2.  Delete the release namespace if one was specified:

    ```ssh
    kubectl delete namespace gateway
    ```

3.  Delete the custom resource definitions:

    ```ssh
    kubectl delete crds \
         apiinstances.gateway.mulesoft.com \
         configurations.gateway.mulesoft.com \
         extensions.gateway.mulesoft.com \
         policybindings.gateway.mulesoft.com \
         services.gateway.mulesoft.com
    ```
---

# Upgrade Self-Managed Omni Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can upgrade Omni Gateway as a Linux service, in a Docker container, or in a Kubernetes cluster.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#linux"><img src="_images/install-linux-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#docker"><img src="_images/install-docker-logo.png" alt="25%" width="25%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#kubernetes"><img src="_images/install-kubernetes-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#linux">Upgrade as a Linux Service</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#docker">Upgrade in a Docker Container</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#kubernetes">Upgrade in a Kubernetes Cluster</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Upgrade as a Linux Service

Omni Gateway is distributed as a standard package. Use the following steps to upgrade or downgrade
Omni Gateway to either a specific version or the latest version.

To upgrade or downgrade Omni Gateway as a Linux Service:

1.  Update the list of available packages:

    ```ssh
    sudo apt update
    ```

    To list the available versions, run:

    ```ssh
    apt list -a flex-gateway
    ```

2.  Update Omni Gateway:
    - To the latest version:

      ```ssh
      sudo apt --only-upgrade -y install flex-gateway
      ```

    - To a specific version:

      ```ssh
      sudo apt --only-upgrade -y install flex-gateway=<version>
      ```

    - To a downgrade version:

      ```ssh
      sudo apt --only-upgrade -y --allow-downgrades install flex-gateway=<version>
      ```

3.  Restart the services:

    ```ssh
    sudo systemctl restart flex-gateway
    ```

## Upgrade in a Docker Container

The docker containers are not updated, if a new version is needed then a new container with the new
image version should be created to replace the outdated one.

The new versions of the images will be available in
[mulesoft/flex-gateway](https://hub.docker.com/r/mulesoft/flex-gateway).

## Upgrade in a Kubernetes Cluster

1.  Update charts with the respective chart repositories:

    ```ssh
    helm repo up
    ```

2.  Upgrade the release to a new version of the Helm chart by executing the following command,
    replacing example information with your own:

    ```ssh
    helm upgrade -n <namespace> <release_name> flex-gateway/flex-gateway --wait
    ```
---

# Omni Gateway Version Lifecycle

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Starting with Omni Gateway 1.9, MuleSoft introduces two new release channels, Edge and Long-term
Support (LTS). These release channels are available for both Managed and Self-Managed Omni Gateway.

## Edge and LTS Releases for Omni Gateway

Consider the information in this table to determine which release channel best suits your
requirements.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Channel Type</th><th>Release Cadence</th><th>Differentiators</th><th>Support Coverage</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Edge</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Three times per year</p></div><div><ul><li><p>May</p></li><li><p>August</p></li><li><p>November</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Includes new features more often and has a shorter support period for each release.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Omni Gateway 1.9 and later</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Five months Standard Support</p></li><li><p>Three months Extended Support</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Omni Gateway 1.8 and earlier</p></div><div><ul><li><p>See <a href="https://www.salesforce.com/content/dam/web/en_us/www/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf">MuleSoft Support Policy</a>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>LTS</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Once a year</p></div><div><ul><li><p>February</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bundles new features in an annual release and has a longer support period for each release.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Omni Gateway 1.9 and later</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Eighteen months Standard Support</p></li><li><p>Six months Extended Support</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Omni Gateway 1.8 and earlier</p></div><div><ul><li><p>See <a href="https://www.salesforce.com/content/dam/web/en_us/www/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf">MuleSoft Support Policy</a>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Release dates are subject to change due to product needs.

For detailed information and support timeframes for Omni Gateway 1.8 and earlier, see the
[MuleSoft Support Policy](https://www.salesforce.com/content/dam/web/en_us/www/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf).

## Omni Gateway Version Support

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Omni Gateway Version</th><th>Release Date</th><th>End of Standard Support</th><th>End of Extended Support</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>1.13.0 Edge</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>May 11, 2026</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>October 11, 2026</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>January 11, 2028</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>1.12.0 LTS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>February 25, 2026</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>August 25, 2027</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>February 25, 2028</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>1.11.0 Edge</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>October 15, 2025</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>March 15, 2026</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>June 15, 2026</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>1.10.0 Edge</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>June 27, 2025</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>November 27, 2025</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>February 27, 2026</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>1.9.0 LTS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>March 18, 2025</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>September 18, 2026</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>March 18, 2027</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Deployment Models and Lifecycle Management

Omni Gateway lifecycle management varies depending on the release channel and the type of gateway.
Managed Omni Gateway instances are auto-upgraded regularly, and any available patches are applied
once a month. Self-managed gateways are never patched or upgraded automatically.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Managed Gateway</th><th>Self-Managed Gateway</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Auto-upgrade</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Managed Gateways are auto-upgraded to the latest minor version within their respective channels (Edge or LTS).</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Edge: Three times per year</p></li><li><p>LTS: Once a year</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No auto-upgrade capabilities</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Patches</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Monthly auto-patching</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Available for Omni Gateway versions that are within the support period for their respective channels (Edge or LTS).</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Self-upgrade</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You can manually upgrade to the latest version that matches your release channel (Edge or LTS) before the scheduled automatic upgrade.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Always available</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Rollback</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You can revert to the previous version if itâs still within the release channel support period.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Available to the previously used version</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Restart</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Always</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Until End of Extended Support</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Keep running</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Always</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Until End of Extended Support</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Supportability</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard Support (auto-upgrade ensures that managed gateways are always on the latest Edge or LTS version).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Until End of Extended Support. See the <a href="https://www.salesforce.com/content/dam/web/en_us/www/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf">MuleSoft Support Policy</a> for more information.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

For information on patching, upgrading, rolling back, or deleting a gateway, see
[Maintaining an Omni Gateway](flex-gateway-maintain).
---

# Downloading Omni Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Download and install Omni Gateway as a Linux service, or download the Omni Gateway image from the
container registry. Downloading is a prerequisite to registering and running a gateway as a Linux
service or in a container, Kubernetes cluster, or OpenShift cluster.

To run Omni Gateway inside a virtual machine (VM), download and install it as a Linux service. To
run Omni Gateway in a container, Kubernetes cluster, or OpenShift cluster, download the Omni Gateway
image.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#linux"><img src="_images/install-linux-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#container"><img src="_images/install-container-logo.png" alt="25%" width="25%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#kubernetes"><img src="_images/install-kubernetes-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><span><a href="#openshift"><img src="_images/install-openshift-logo.png" alt="20%" width="20%"></a></span></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#linux">Linux</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#container">Container</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#kubernetes">Kubernetes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#openshift">OpenShift</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

See [Requirements and Limits for Omni Gateway](flex-review-prerequisites) for details about hardware
and software requirements.

## Download and Install as a Linux Service

You can download and install Omni Gateway as a service in one of the following Linux environments:

#### ![2%](_images/install-ubuntu-logo.png) Ubuntu

Download and install Omni Gateway as an Ubuntu service

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) versions of Ubuntu:

- Ubuntu Jammy

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)
- [lsb_release](https://refspecs.linuxfoundation.org/LSB_3.0.0/LSB-PDA/LSB-PDA/lsbrelease.html)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as a Ubuntu service, run the command block that performs the
following actions:

- Retrieves the public package keys and adds the package repository
- Updates the package list
- Updates `apt-get`
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
curl -XGET https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/ubuntu/pubkey.gpg | sudo apt-key add -
echo "deb [arch=amd64] https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mulesoft.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y flex-gateway
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

#### ![2%](_images/install-debian-logo.png) Debian

Download and install Omni Gateway as a Debian service

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) versions of Debian:

- Debian Bookworm

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)
- [lsb_release](https://refspecs.linuxfoundation.org/LSB_3.0.0/LSB-PDA/LSB-PDA/lsbrelease.html)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as a Debian service, run the command block that performs the
following actions:

- Retrieves the public package keys and adds the package repository
- Updates the package list
- Updates `apt-get`
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
curl -XGET https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/debian/pubkey.gpg | sudo apt-key add -
echo "deb [arch=amd64] https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/debian $(lsb_release -cs) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mulesoft.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y flex-gateway
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

#### ![2%](_images/install-amazon-linux-logo.png) Amazon Linux

Download and install Omni Gateway as an Amazon Linux service

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) version of Amazon Linux:

- Amazon Linux 2023

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as an Amazon Linux service, run the command block that performs
the following actions:

- Adds the Omni Gateway package repository with a reference to its public key
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/flex-gateway.repo <<\EOF
[flex-gateway]
name = Anypoint Flex Gateway
baseurl = https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/amazonlinux/2023/main
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/amazonlinux/pubkey.gpg
repo_gpgcheck=1
enabled=1
EOF

sudo yum install -y flex-gateway
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

#### ![2%](_images/install-rhel-logo.png) Red Hat Enterprise Linux

Download and install Omni Gateway as a Red Hat Enterprise Linux service

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) versions of RHEL:

- Red Hat Enterprise Linux (9)

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as a Red Hat Enterprise Linux service, run the command block
that performs the following actions:

- Adds the Omni Gateway package repository with a reference to its public key
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/flex-gateway.repo <<\EOF
[flex-gateway]
name = Anypoint Flex Gateway
baseurl = https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/rhel/$releasever/main
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/rhel/pubkey.gpg
repo_gpgcheck=1
enabled=1
EOF

sudo yum install -y flex-gateway
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

#### ![6%](_images/install-rhel-ibm-logo.png) Red Hat Enterprise Linux on IBM Power

Download and install Omni Gateway as a Red Hat Enterprise Linux service on IBM Power

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) versions:

- Red Hat Enterprise Linux (9) on IBM Power (ppc64le)

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as a Red Hat Enterprise Linux service, run the command block
that performs the following actions:

- Adds the Omni Gateway package repository with a reference to its public key
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/flex-gateway.repo <<\EOF
[flex-gateway]
name = Anypoint Flex Gateway
baseurl = https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/rhel/$releasever/main
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/rhel/pubkey.gpg
repo_gpgcheck=1
enabled=1
EOF

sudo yum install -y flex-gateway
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

#### ![2%](_images/install-centos-logo.png) CentOS

Download and install Omni Gateway as a CentOS service

##### Software Requirements

Omni Gateway runs on the following Long Term Support (LTS) version of CentOS:

- CentOS 8

##### Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have installed the following:

- [gnupg2](https://gnupg.org/)

##### Run Installation Commands

To download and install Omni Gateway as an CentOS service, run the command block that performs the
following actions:

- Adds the Omni Gateway package repository with a reference to its public key
- Installs the Omni Gateway package

```ssh
sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/flex-gateway.repo <<\EOF
[flex-gateway]
name = Anypoint Flex Gateway
baseurl = https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/centos/$releasever/main
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://flex-packages.anypoint.mulesoft.com/centos/pubkey.gpg
repo_gpgcheck=1
enabled=1
EOF

sudo yum install -y flex-gateway
```

Due to issues with the CentOS package manager you might encounter the following error:

Error: Failed to download metadata for repo 'AppStream': Cannot prepare internal mirrorlist: No URLs
in mirrorlist

If you encounter this error, run the following command:

```ssh
sudo sed -i -e "s|mirrorlist=|#mirrorlist=|g" -e "s|#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g" /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-*
```

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run). For more detail, see
[Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

## Download Omni Gateway for Use in a Container

You can download and install Omni Gateway in one of the following containers:

#### ![2%](_images/install-docker-logo.png) Docker

Download Omni Gateway for Use in a Docker Container

Download the Omni Gateway container image to prepare for registering and running a gateway in a
Docker container:

1.  Download the Omni Gateway Docker image:

    ```ssh
    docker pull mulesoft/flex-gateway:latest
    ```

2.  Verify that the download succeeded:

    ```ssh
    docker images mulesoft/flex-gateway
    ```

    When successful, the command lists the Omni Gateway container image, for example:

    ```text
    REPOSITORY                   TAG
    mulesoft/flex-gateway        latest
    ```

For deployments using Docker images, add the Omni Gateway Docker image to your Docker registry to
ensure the image is available in the event of a Dockerhub outage.

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run).

For more detail, see [Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).

Podman container:

    ```ssh

    ```

2.  Verify that the download succeeded:

    ```ssh

    ```

    When successful, the command lists the Omni Gateway container image, for example:

    ```text
    REPOSITORY                   TAG

    ```

For deployments using Docker images, add the Omni Gateway Docker image to your Docker registry to
ensure the image is available in the event of a Dockerhub outage.

After downloading and installing, register Omni Gateway in [Connected](flex-conn-reg-run) or
[Local Mode](flex-local-reg-run).

For more detail, see [Connected Mode vs. Local Mode](./#connected-mode-vs-local-mode).
