# External Authorization Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy name</strong></p></td><td><p>External Authorization</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Authenticates requests with an external gRPC or HTTP authorization service</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Security</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.6.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>403 - Forbidden, invalid client application credentials</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The External Authorization policy forwards incoming client HTTP requests to an external gRPC or HTTP
authorization service to determine if the client HTTP request is authorized to access the upstream
service. If the request is authorized, Omni Gateway allows the request to pass to the upstream
service. For unauthorized accesses, Omni Gateway returns a `403` forbidden request error.

To forward request headers, such as an authorization token header, from the client HTTP request to
the authorization service, configure the **Allowed Headers** parameter. Additionally, for HTTP
authorization servers, use the **Headers to add** parameter to inject new headers into the
authorization request.

> [!IMPORTANT] Omni Gateway sends headers to the authorization service encoded in bytes. To send
> headers encoded in utf8, set the environment variable `FLEX_HEADER_RAW_VALUE_ENABLE=false`.

The External Authorization policy can also modify the client HTTP request and response headers with
additional information returned from the authorization service. For example, if an authorized user
makes a request with an access token header that corresponds to a username, the authorization
service can return a `username` header for Omni Gateway to append to the client HTTP request.

For gRPC authorization servers, if the authorization service returns headers with the authorization
response, Omni Gateway either appends these headers to the client HTTP request or overwrites the
header if it is present in the client request.

For HTTP authorization servers, you can configure what headers to append, overwrite, or ignore.
Policies with an HTTP server configured can also modify the client response returned from the
upstream service.

The External Authorization policy is an extension of the External Authorization filter native to
Envoy. To learn more about the External Authorization filter, see
[External Authorization Filter](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/ext_authz_filter).
Not all External Authorization filter parameters are available to the External Authorization policy.
To find what parameters are configurable, see
[Configuring Policy Parameters](#configuring-policy-parameters).

> [!NOTE] If other policies modify the incoming HTTP request, ensure that you order the policies
> depending on if these modification must be present before sending the request to the authorization
> service. For more information about ordering policies, see [Ordering Policies](policies-reorder).

## Configuring Policy Parameters

To configure your policy parameters, see the following parameter references for the mode your Omni
Gateway is running in:

- [Omni Gateway Local Mode](#local-mode)
- [Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode](#connected-mode)

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: native-ext-authz-flex
  config:
    uri: <string> // REQUIRED
    serverType: <string> // REQUIRED, default "grpc"
    serverApiVersion: <string> // REQUIRED, default `v3`
    serviceRequestHeadersToAdd: <object-array> // OPTIONAL
      - key: <string>
        value: <string>
    serviceResponseUpstreamHeaders: <string-array> // OPTIONAL
    serviceResponseUpstreamHeadersToAppend: <string-array> // OPTIONAL
    serviceResponseClientHeaders: <string-array> // OPTIONAL
    serviceResponseClientHeadersOnSuccess: <string-array> // OPTIONAL
    pathPrefix: <string> // OPTIONAL
    requestTimeout: <number> // REQUIRED, default `5000`
    includePeerCertificate: <boolean> // REQUIRED, default `false`
    allowedHeaders: <string-array> // OPTIONAL
```

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required or Optional</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>uri</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External authorization service URI. With a gRPC type server, define the URI by using this format: <code>h2://&lt;ext.authz.com&gt;:&lt;port&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serverType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>grpc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of external server. Supported values are <code>grpc</code> and <code>http</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serverApiVersion</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>v3</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Transport protocol API version. Supported values are <code>v3</code> and <code>v2</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>includePeerCertificate</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, includes peer certificates in the authorization server request. Supported values are <code>true</code> and <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceRequestHeadersToAdd</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines custom headers and their value to inject into the authorization service request. This parameter is available for <code>HTTP</code> type serves.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceRequestHeadersToAdd.key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the new header to add.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceRequestHeadersToAdd.value</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value of the new header to add.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceResponseUpstreamHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the headers from the authorization response to add to the client request to the upstream service. Omni Gateway overwrites coexisting headers. This parameter is available for <code>HTTP</code> type serves.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceResponseUpstreamHeadersToAppend</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the headers from the authorization response to add to the client request to the upstream service. Omni Gateway appends coexisting headers. This parameter is available for <code>HTTP</code> type serves.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceResponseClientHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the headers from the authorization response to add to the client response returned from the upstream service. Omni Gateway overwrites existing headers. This parameter is available for <code>HTTP</code> type serves.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>serviceResponseClientHeadersOnSuccess</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the headers from the authorization response to add to the client response returned from the upstream service if the request is successfully authorized. Omni Gateway overwrites existing headers. This parameter is available for <code>HTTP</code> type serves.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>pathPrefix</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a prefix to the value of the <code>Path</code> authorization request header if present.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>requestTimeout</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>5000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum time in milliseconds the policy waits for a response from the authentication service.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>allowedHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the headers from the client HTTP request to propagate to authorization request. By default, requests sent to gRPC servers include all client headers. Requests sent to HTTP servers donât include any client headers. If you list allowed headers for either server type, requests include only the listed headers.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Leaving a required value blank configures the parameter as the default value. No value is
> configured for optional values left blank.

### Resource Configuration Example

In the following configuration, a gRPC authorization service receives a request that includes the
peer certificates and authorization token header:

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: native-ext-authz-flex
  config:
    uri: h2://ext-authz-grpc-service:9001
    serverType: "grpc"
    includePeerCertificate: "true"
    allowedHeaders:
      - access-token
```

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Parameter</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>External authorization service URI</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External authorization service URI. With a gRPC type server, define the URI as <code>\h2://&lt;ext.authz.com&gt;:&lt;port&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>External authorization server type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of external server.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Server API version</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Transport protocol API version.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Include peer certificates in the authorization server request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Includes peer certificates in the authorization server request.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path prefix</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a prefix to the value of the <code>Path</code> authorization request header if present.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Request timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum time in milliseconds the policy waits for a response from the authentication service. By default, the request timeout is 5000 milliseconds.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Allowed headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>By default, requests sent to gRPC servers include all client headers and requests sent to HTTP servers donât include any client headers. If you list allowed headers for either server type, requests include only the listed headers.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## How This Policy Works

The following diagram demonstrates the order in which requests and responses flow during the
External Authorization policy execution:

![A sequence diagram that illustrates the interaction between a user, an API gateway, a database, and an API](_images/ext-auth-policy.png)

1.  The client makes an HTTP request to the API instance exposed by Omni Gateway.
2.  Omni Gateway forwards the request consisting of the clientâs request and headers to the
    authorization service.
3.  The authorization services sends the authorization response back to Omni Gateway:
    - If the request isnât authorized, Omni Gateway appends any necessary headers and returns a
      `403` forbidden access code to the client.
    - If the request is authorized, Omni Gateway appends the header data to the client request, if
      necessary, and allows the request to pass to the upstream service.

4.  Omni Gateway appends any necessary headers and returns the response as normal.
---

# External Processing Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy Name</strong></p></td><td><p>External Processing</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Sends the incoming HTTP requests or outgoing HTTP responses requests to an external gRPC service for additional processing</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Transformation</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.6.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>403 - IP is rejected</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The External Processing policy forwards the incoming HTTP request or outgoing HTTP response to an
external gRPC service to modify the headers, trailers, body, or any combination of the three. You
can also configure this policy to process either the request, response, or both.

Additionally, the external processing service can request different parts of the HTTP request or
response after the initial gRPC request was sent by Omni Gateway to the external processing server.
This enables you to send the minimum amount of data necessary. For example, Omni Gateway can
initially send only the HTTP requests headers. Then, after some processing, the external processor
can request the HTTP request body or trailers from Omni Gateway.

The parameters you set for sending the HTTP headers, trailers, and body only configure what is
initially sent. The external processing service has access to all parts of the HTTP request or
response regardless of what is configured. Configure the parameters to send all of the information
that the processing service needs for every incoming HTTP request or response. For example, if the
processing service needs the headers for every request but only needs the body for some of the
requests, depending on the information found in the header, only configure the parameters to send
the headers.

The External Processing policy is an extension of the External Processing filter native to Envoy. To
learn more about the External Processing filter, see
[External Processing Filter](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto).
Not all External Processing filter parameters are available for the External Processing policy. To
find what parameters are configurable, see
[Configuring Policy Parameters](#configuring-policy-parameters).

> [!NOTE] If other policies modify the HTTP request or response, ensure that you order the policies
> depending on if these modification should be present when the request is sent to the processing
> service or upstream service. For more information about ordering policies, see
> [Ordering Policies](policies-reorder).

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: native-ext-proc-flex
  config:
    uri: <string> // REQUIRED
    messageTimeout: <integer> // OPTIONAL, default: 1000
    requestHeaderMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "send"
    responseHeaderMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "send"
    requestBodyMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "none"
    responseBodyMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "none"
    requestTrailerMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "skip"
    responseTrailerMode: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: "skip"
    failureModeAllow: <boolean> // OPTIONAL, default: false
    maxMessageTimeout: <integer> // OPTIONAL, default: 0
    allowModeOverride: <boolean> // OPTIONAL, default: true
```

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required or Optional</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>uri</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NA</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External processing server URI. For gRPC type servers, define the URI as <code>\h2://&lt;ext.authz.com&gt;:&lt;port&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>messageTimeout</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message timeout for the processing server in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>maxMessageTimeout</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the upper bound the processing server can override <code>messageTimeout</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>failureModeAllow</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When <code>true</code>, if the gRPC stream prematurely closes or canât be opened, request processing continues without error.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>allowModeOverride</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the processing server to override the policy configuration. When <code>true</code>, the server can override the configuration of the <code>requestHeaderMode</code>, <code>responseHeaderMode</code>, <code>requestBodyMode</code>, <code>responseBodyMode</code>, <code>requestTrailerMode</code>, and <code>responseTrailerMode</code> parameters.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>observabilityMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When <code>true</code>, the configured headers, body, and trailers are sent to the external processing server and Omni Gateway doesnât wait for a response from the processing server.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>requestHeaderMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>send</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the HTTP requests headers are sent to the processing service. Supported values are <code>send</code> and <code>skip</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>responseHeaderMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>send</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the HTTP response headers are sent to the processing service. Supported values are <code>send</code> and <code>skip</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>requestBodyMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>none</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mode that the HTTP request body is sent in. Supported values are <code>none</code>, <code>streamed</code>, <code>buffered</code>, and <code>bufferedPartial</code>. Selecting <code>none</code> does not send the request body.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>responseBodyMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>none</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mode that the HTTP response body is sent in. Supported values are <code>none</code>, <code>streamed</code>, <code>buffered</code>, and <code>bufferedPartial</code>. Selecting <code>none</code> does not send the request body.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>requestTrailerMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>skip</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the HTTP requests' trailer headers are sent to the processing service. Supported values are <code>send</code> and <code>skip</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>responseTrailerMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>skip</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the HTTP response trailer headers are sent to the processing service. Supported values are <code>send</code> and <code>skip</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Leaving a value blank configures the parameter with the default value. If a required
> parameter doesnât have a default value, you must configure the parameter.

#### Resource Configuration Example

This configuration streams the request headers, trailers, and body to the external processing
server. This configuration does not send any part of the response to the external processing server.

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: native-ext-proc-flex
  config:
    uri: h2://<ext.authz.com>:<port>
    messageTimeout: <integer> // OPTIONAL
    responseHeaderMode: skip
    requestBodyMode: streamed
    responseTrailerMode: skip
```

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>External processing server URI</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External processing server URI. For gRPC type servers, define the URI as <code>\h2://&lt;ext.authz.com&gt;:&lt;port&gt;</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message timeout for the processing server in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Max message timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the upper bound the processing server can override <strong>Message timeout</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Failure mode allow</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When <code>true</code>, if the gRPC stream prematurely closes or canât be opened, request processing continues without error.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Allow mode override</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the processing server to override the policy configuration. When <code>true</code>, the server can override the configuration of the <strong>Request header processing mode</strong>, <strong>Response header processing mode</strong>, <strong>Request body processing mode</strong>, <strong>Response body processing mode</strong>, <strong>Request trailer processing mode</strong>, and <strong>Response trailer processing mode</strong> parameters.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Observability mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled, the configured headers, body, and trailers are sent to the external processing server and Omni Gateway doesnât wait for a response from the processing server.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Request header processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the requests headers are sent to the processing service.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Response header processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the response headers are sent to the processing service.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Request body processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mode that the request body is sent in.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Response body processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mode that the response body is sent in.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Request trailer processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the requests trailers are sent to the processing service.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Response trailer processing mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the response trailers are sent to the processing service.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Leaving a value blank configures the parameter with the default value. If a required
> parameter doesnât have a default value, you must configure the parameter.

## How This Policy Works

The following diagram demonstrates the order in which requests and responses flow when the External
Processing policy is applied:

![A flowchart illustrating API interaction](_images/ext-processing-policy.png)

1.  The client makes an HTTP request to Omni Gateway.
2.  Omni Gateway forwards the initial parts of the client HTTP request that you configure to the
    external processing service. If **Observability mode** is enabled, the next step is skipped.
3.  Omni Gateway awaits the modified client HTTP request. If **Allow mode override** is enabled, the
    external processing service can request additional parts of the request.
4.  Omni Gateway sends the client HTTP request to the upstream API service and awaits the HTTP
    response.
5.  Omni Gateway forwards the initial parts of the HTTP response that you configure to the external
    processing service. If **Observability mode** is enabled, the next step is skipped.
6.  Omni Gateway awaits the modified HTTP response. If **Allow mode override** is enabled, the
    external processing service can request additional parts of the HTTP response.
7.  Omni Gateway returns the modified HTTP response from the external processing service to the
    client.

By default, if Omni Gateway canât establish a connection to the gRPC service or if the gRPC service
returns an error, the policy fails. To forward requests regardless of connection or error status,
enable the **Failure mode allow** parameter.
---

# GraphQL Operation Limits Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy Name</strong></p></td><td><p>GraphQL Operation Limits</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Limits GraphQL operation depth, aliases, root fields, and directives</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Security</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.13.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned status codes</strong></p></td><td><p>400 - Operations that exceed a configured limit. The response follows the standard GraphQL error format and lists each violation. For example, <code>Query depth of 7 exceeds the maximum allowed depth of 5</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The GraphQL Operation Limits policy restricts the depth, aliases, root fields, and directives in
incoming requests. If an operation exceeds a limit, Omni Gateway returns a 400 error with a JSON
`errors` array detailing each violation.

Omni Gateway evaluates limits at parse time from the document text. Because fragment spreads donât
expand, metrics reflect the written document instead of a fully expanded query plan that the gateway
builds at runtime.

For persisted queries, the gateway resolves the document without inline query text. The policy
bypasses these requests and allows them to pass to the upstream service.

If the parser canât read the query, the policy logs a debug message and doesnât block the request,
so upstream validation or other policies can still run.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: graphql-operation-limits-flex config: maxDepth: <integer> // OPTIONAL, use -1 or
omit to disable maxAliases: <integer> // OPTIONAL, use -1 or omit to disable maxRootFields:
<integer> // OPTIONAL, use -1 or omit to disable maxDirectives: <integer> // OPTIONAL, use -1 or
omit to disable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required</th><th>Description</th><th><code>maxDepth</code></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Omit or set to <code>-1</code> to disable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum depth of nested fields in a single document.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>maxAliases</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Omit or set to <code>-1</code> to disable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of field aliases.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>maxRootFields</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Omit or set to <code>-1</code> to disable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of top-level fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>maxDirectives</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Resource Configuration Example

This example caps depth and aliases while leaving root fields and directives unrestricted.

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: graphql-operation-limits-flex
  config:
    maxDepth: 8
    maxAliases: 20
    maxRootFields: -1
    maxDirectives: -1
```

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Maximum Depth</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Deepest level of field nesting in a document. For example, <code>{ hero { name } }</code> has a depth of 2.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Maximum Aliases</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of field aliases in the document.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Maximum Root Fields</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of top-level fields that an operation can declare.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Maximum Directives</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of directives across the document.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Inbound Policies Directory](policies-included-directory)
- [Securing Omni Gateway Instances with Policies](flex-gateway-secure-apis)

# GraphQL Schema Validation Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy Name</strong></p></td><td><p>GraphQL Schema Validation</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Validates incoming GraphQL operations against a GraphQL schema definition</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Security</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.13.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned status codes</strong></p></td><td><p>400 - The operation doesnât satisfy the schema. When <strong>Block request</strong> is enabled, the JSON response includes GraphQL-style <code>errors</code> with a validation message.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The GraphQL Schema Validation policy applies standard GraphQL validation rules, including field and
argument correctness, fragments, directives, unions, interfaces, and other specification rules.

The policy loads the schema in these ways:

- If `source` is `context`, Flex Gateway automatically loads the schema file from the API asset in
  Anypoint Exchange.
- If `source` is `inline`, provide the schema text directly in the policy configuration. This is
  required for local mode or environments not connected to Exchange.

If the document includes an `@link` to the Apollo Federation specification URL, Omni Gateway uses
this schema to validate all incoming requests.

After Omni Gateway extracts inline GraphQL operation text from the request, the policy runs
validation. If Omni Gateway canât resolve normal operation text, such as persisted query flows, it
skips these checks and logs a warning before it forwards the request to the upstream.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: graphql-schema-validation-flex config: source: <string> // OPTIONAL, default:
context; values: "context" or "inline" schema: <string> // REQUIRED when source is inline; omit when
source is context blockOperation: <boolean> // OPTIONAL, default: true

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>source</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>context</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Where Omni Gateway retrieves the schema for validation. If set to <code>context</code>, Omni Gateway automatically loads the schema file from the API asset in Anypoint Exchange. If set to <code>inline</code>, provide the schema text directly in the policy configuration. This is required for local mode or environments not connected to Exchange.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>schema</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NA</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The schema text used for validation. Required when <code>source</code> is set to <code>inline</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>blockOperation</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How Omni Gateway handles requests that fail validation. If <code>true</code>, Omni Gateway blocks the request and returns a <code>400</code> Bad Request. If <code>false</code>, Omni Gateway logs a warning and forwards the request to your backend.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Resource Configuration Example

This example validates against a minimal inline schema:

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: graphql-schema-validation-flex
  config:
    source: inline
    blockOperation: true
    schema: |
      type Query {
        hello: String
      }
```

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Block request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled and the operation doesnât conform to the schema, Omni Gateway returns a <code>400</code> error. If disabled, Omni Gateway logs a warning and forwards the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Inbound Policies Directory](policies-included-directory)
- [Schema Validation Policy](policies-included-schema-validation)
- [Securing Omni Gateway Instances with Policies](flex-gateway-secure-apis)

# GraphQL Static Query Complexity Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy Name</strong></p></td><td><p>GraphQL Static Query Complexity</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Calculates query complexity and blocks requests that exceed the defined budget</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Security</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.13.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned status codes</strong></p></td><td><p>400 - Parse failures always trigger a 400 response. Complexity and list violations return this code only if <strong>Block request</strong> is enabled. The response includes a standard GraphQL errors object.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The GraphQL Static Query Complexity policy estimates the complexity of each incoming GraphQL
operation from your GraphQL schema and the operation text. If the estimate exceeds the configured
budget or fails other checks enforced by the policy, Omni Gateway rejects the request or logs a
warning and forwards it upstream.

The policy loads schema in these ways:

- If `source` is `context`, Omni Gateway automatically loads the schema file from the API asset in
  Anypoint Exchange.
- If `source` is `inline`, provide the schema text directly in the policy configuration. This is
  required for local mode or environments not connected to Exchange.

After Omni Gateway extracts the GraphQL operation text, the policy analyzes query complexity.

If Omni Gateway canât resolve the full operation text, as with persisted queries, it skips
complexity analysis.

If a system error occurs during analysis, even if the schema loads into memory, Omni Gateway logs a
warning and forwards the request instead of blocking it.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: graphql-static-query-complexity-flex config: maximumComplexity: <number> //
REQUIRED, must be positive source: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: context; values: "context" or
"inline" schema: <string> // REQUIRED when source is inline; omit when source is context
defaultFieldCost: <number> // OPTIONAL, default: 1.0, must be >= 0 blockOperation: <boolean> //
OPTIONAL, default: true rejectUnboundedLists: <boolean> // OPTIONAL, default: true directiveName:
<string> // OPTIONAL, default: cost valueArgument: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: weight
multipliersArgument: <string> // OPTIONAL, default: multipliers

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>maximumComplexity</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum complexity score allowed for a single query. Requests exceeding this value are blocked or logged as warnings.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>source</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>context</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>context</code> automatically retrieves the schema file from the API asset in Anypoint Exchange. <code>inline</code> uses the schema text provided directly in the policy configuration. This is required for local mode or environments not connected to Exchange.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>schema</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NA</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The schema text used for validation. Required if <code>source</code> is <code>inline</code>. Omit for <code>context</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>defaultFieldCost</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1.0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The cost applied to fields that donât have a specific complexity tag (typically @cost) in your schema file.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>blockOperation</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether Omni Gateway blocks requests that exceed the complexity limit. If <code>true</code>, Omni Gateway blocks the request and returns a <code>400</code> Bad Request error. If <code>false</code>, Omni Gateway logs a warning and forwards the request to your backend. Parse failures always trigger a <code>400</code> response, even if this setting is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>rejectUnboundedLists</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Omni Gateway blocks queries for list fields that lack a limiting argument, such as <code>first</code> or <code>limit</code>. This prevents users from requesting large datasets.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>directiveName</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>cost</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the complexity tag in your schema file that defines per-field costs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>valueArgument</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>weight</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The specific argument within your shema tag that defines the cost of a field. For example, in <code>@cost(value: 5)</code>, the argument is <code>value</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>multipliersArgument</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>multipliers</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The argument in your shcema tag that lists which field arguments multiply the cost. For example, in <code>@cost(multipliers: ["first"])</code>, the value of the <code>first</code> argument is used to multiply the fieldâs cost.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Resource Configuration Example

This example sets the maximum complexity at `250`, uses default costs and tag names, and provides
the schema text inline:

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: graphql-static-query-complexity-flex
  config:
    source: inline
    maximumComplexity: 250
    blockOperation: true
    rejectUnboundedLists: true
    schema: |
      directive @cost(weight: Int!, multipliers: [String!]) on FIELD_DEFINITION

      type Query {
        users(limit: Int): [User!]! @cost(weight: 1, multipliers: ["limit"])
        version: String!
      }

      type User {
        id: ID!
        name: String!
      }
```

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Maximum Complexity</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum allowed complexity score for a query.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Default Field Cost</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The cost applied to fields lacking a specific complexity tag in your schema file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1.0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Block request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether Omni Gateway blocks requests that exceed the complexity limit. If enabled, Omni Gateway blocks the request and returns a <code>400</code> error. If disabled, Omni Gateway logs violations as warnings. Parse failures always trigger a <code>400</code> response, regardless of this setting.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Reject unbounded lists</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled, Omni Gateway blocks queries for list fields that lack a limiting argument, such as <code>first</code> or <code>limit</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Directive Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the complexity tag in your schema file that defines per-field costs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>cost</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Value Argument</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The argument within your complexity tag that specifies the fieldâs base cost.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>weight</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Multipliers Argument</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The argument within your complexity tag that identifies which query parameters multiply the cost. For example, in \@cost(weight: 1, multipliers: ["limit"])<code>, the multipliers argument is `multipliers</code>, and the <code>limit</code> value multiplies the base cost.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>multipliers</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Header Injection Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy name</strong></p></td><td><p>Header Injection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Adds headers to a request or response.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Transformation</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.0.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>500 - The configuration includes an expression that was not evaluated.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The Header Injection policy adds HTTP headers to the request or response of a message. When you
configure this policy for your API, you must specify an inbound and outbound map of the headers that
you want to add in the message processing in the form of a key-value pair.

![A flow chart illustrating the process of setting header injection and removal policies for clients accessing an API through a proxy](_images/header-inject-remove-diagram.png)

You can optionally include DataWeave expressions in the value or name of the header. For example,
all headers matching the following configured expression are injected to the message:

`â#[attributes.requestPath]â`

If the injected header already exists in the message attributes, the policy creates a new header
without overriding the value of the existing one.

> [!NOTE] Only certain headers are restricted to support multiple values, and they will be
> overwritten using configured values. These headers are: `access-control-allow-origin`,
> `content-type`, `content-length`, `transfer-encoding`.
>
> To overwrite any other existing message header, remove it before adding your header. See the
> [Header Removal](policies-included-header-removal) policy.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: header-injection-flex config: inboundHeaders: <array> // OPTIONAL, default:
\[\] - key: <string> // REQUIRED value: <string> // REQUIRED outboundHeaders: <array> // OPTIONAL,
default: \[\] - key: <string> // REQUIRED value: <string> // REQUIRED

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required or Optional</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>inboundHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Empty array</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of headers to be injected at the beginning of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>inboundHeaders.key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A string or DataWeave expression for header name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>inboundHeaders.value</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A string or DataWeave expression for header value</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>outboundHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Empty array</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of header to be injected at the end of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>outboundHeaders.key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A string or DataWeave expression for header name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>outboundHeaders.value</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A string or DataWeave expression for header value</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] `inboundHeaders` and `outboundHeaders` can not both be empty.

#### Resource Configuration Example

In the following example, all the headers matching the configured `#[attributes.requestPath]`
expression are injected into the message.

\- policyRef: name: header-injection-flex config: inboundHeaders: - key: "new-inbound-header" value:
"#\[attributes.requestPath\]" outboundHeaders: - key: "new-outbound-header" value:
"#\[attributes.requestPath\]"

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Parameter</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Header Map</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of headers to be injected at the beginning of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Header Key</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String or Dataweave expression to be used as header name at the beginning of the message.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Header Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String or Dataweave expression to be used as header value at the beginning of the message.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Outbound Header Map</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of headers to be injected at the end of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Outbound Header Key</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String or Dataweave expression to be used as header name at the end of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Outbound Header Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String or Dataweave expression to be used as header value at the end of the message processing.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method &amp; Resource conditions</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The option to add configurations to only a select few or all methods and resources of the API</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Header keys should be compliance with
> [HTTP 1.1](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec2.html#sec2.2) standard.

# Header Removal Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy name</strong></p></td><td><p>Header Removal</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Removes headers from a request or a response</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Transformation</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.0.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>500 - The configuration includes an expression that was not evaluated.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The Header Removal policy removes all listed headers from a request or a response of a message when
you specify an inbound and outbound map of the headers that you want to remove with a key-value
pair.

![A flow chart illustrating the process of setting header injection and removal policies for clients accessing an API through a proxy](_images/header-inject-remove-diagram.png)

You can optionally include DataWeave expressions in the value of the header. For example, all
headers matching the following configured expression are injected to the message:

`â#[attributes.requestPath]â`

> [!NOTE] The following headers may not be removed: accept, host, content-type, content-length,
> date, transfer-encoding, connection,

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: header-removal-flex config: inboundHeaders: <array> // OPTIONAL, default: \[\]
outboundHeaders: <array> // OPTIONAL, default: \[\]

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required or Optional</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>inboundHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Empty array</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of header names or regular expressions to be removed at the beginning of the message processing, for example, <code>Cache-Control, Access-Control.*</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>outboundHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Empty array</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of header names or regular expressions to be removed at the end of the message processing, for example, <code>Access-Control-Allow-Credentials</code></p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE"><div><p><code>inboundHeaders</code> and <code>outboundHeaders</code> can not both be empty.</p></div></div></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Resource Configuration Example

\- policyRef: name: header-removal-flex config: inboundHeaders: - "Cache-Control" -
"Access-Control.\*" outboundHeaders: - "Access-Control-Allow-Credentials"

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Parameter</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Header Map</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of header names or regular expressions (comma separated) to be removed at the beginning of the message processing, for example, <code>Cache-Control, Access-Control.*</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Outbound Header Map</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of header names or regular expressions (comma separated) to be removed at the end of the message processing, for example, <code>Access-Control-Allow-Credentials</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method &amp; Resource conditions</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The option to add configurations to only a select few or all methods and resources of the API</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## How This Policy Works

Because every header that matches a Header Removal policy parameter is removed from the message, you
must be careful about how you configure that parameter.

For example, the following parameter configuration removes any header starting with the word
`Access-Control`, such as the `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials` and `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`
headers from a message. The configuration also removes the `Cache-Control` header:

![Removed headers at the start of message processing](_images/remove-inbound-header.png)

Figure 1. Inbound Header
---

# Health Check Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy name</strong></p></td><td><p>Health Check</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Monitors API upstream health at specific intervals</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Quality of Service</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.4.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>No return codes exist for this policy.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The Health Check policy enables you to monitor an API instanceâs connection to an upstream service
and sends you an email alert if the connection status changes. The Health Check policy monitors the
health of the upstream service and not the health of the API instance or Omni Gateway.

The Health Check policy monitors your API by making GET requests to the specified upstreamâs base
path. You must specify a base path with a GET request. Requests are made at a fixed schedule every
minute with the first request. The first request occurs a minute after the policy is applied.
Requests made by the Health Check policy do not count as Omni Gateway API calls for pricing
purposes.

When you configure Health Check, specify a response code for the GET request that indicates a
healthy connection. For example, the status code of an active API should return 200, indicating the
API can receive connections. The platform sends an email alert when the status code is not 200.
Health Check only validates the response code, so what is returned to the endpoint is unimportant.

No logging event appears for healthy responses. Omni Gateway logs only unhealthy responses.

You can use any 1xx, 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, or 5xx response codes. However, if you expect successful
connections to your endpoints, 2xx response codes are ideal. Only a number response code is
accepted.

You are required to create an alert for the Health Check policy. Applying the policy navigates you
to the Create Alert page, where you must create an alert. For information about how to configure
your alert, visit [Using Omni Gateway API Alerts](flex-use-api-alerts).

> [!IMPORTANT] Applying the Health Check policy to API Instances with an outbound TLS context also
> applied causes the Health Check policy to return an unhealthy connection.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

The Health Check policy isnât supported in Local Mode.

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Element</th><th>Description</th><th>Required?</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Upstream URL</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The full URL of the API upstream you are monitoring</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Base path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The HTTP path of the GET method that is requested during health checks</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Response code</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The expected status code of the GET request specified in the base path. Only a number response code is accepted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# HTTP Caching Policy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Policy Name</strong></p></td><td><p>HTTP caching</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary</strong></p></td><td><p>Caches HTTP responses from an API implementation</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Category</strong></p></td><td><p>Quality of Service</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>First Omni Gateway version available</strong></p></td><td><p>v1.0.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Returned Status Codes</strong></p></td><td><p>No return codes exist for this policy</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Summary

The HTTP Caching policy enables you to cache HTTP responses for reuse. Caching these responses
speeds up the response time for user requests and reduces the load on the backend. For example, if
your backend exposes an endpoint for which the responses to requests are not likely to change, you
can reuse the HTTP responses and bypass the backend request processing by using the HTTP Caching
policy.

Policies applied after the HTTP Caching policy in the policy order are not applied to responses
served from the cache. For example, a rate limiting policy applied after the HTTP Caching policy
only limits the responses served by the upstream service but not the responses served by the cache.
Apply all policies needed for the cached response before the HTTP Caching policy.

For Self-Managed Gateways, HTTP caching requires that you
[configure shared storage](#configure-shared-storage-for-self-managed-gateway). For Managed
Gateways, Omni Replica shares the cache with each other directly. To learn more about the Managed
Gateway architecture, see
[Managed Omni Gateway High Availability and Disaster Recovery Deployments](flex-architecture-managed-dr-ha).

> [!NOTE] The HTTP Caching policy does not support MCP and A2A server instances.

## Configuring Policy Parameters

### Omni Gateway Local Mode

When you apply the policy via declarative configuration files, Refer to the following policy
definition and table of parameters:

\- policyRef: name: http-caching-flex config: httpCachingKey: <string> // REQUIRED, default:
"#\[attributes.requestPath\]" maxCacheEntries: <int> // REQUIRED, default: 10000 ttl: <int> //
REQUIRED, default: 600 useHttpCacheHeaders: <bool> // REQUIRED, default: true invalidationHeader:
<string> // OPTIONAL, default: "" requestExpression: <string> // OPTIONAL, default:
"#\[attributes.method == 'GET' or attributes.method == 'HEAD'\]" responseExpression: <string> //
OPTIONAL, default: "#\[\[200, 203, 204, 206, 300, 301, 404, 405, 410, 414, 501\] contains
attributes.statusCode\]" distributed: <bool> // REQUIRED, default: false persistCache: <bool> //
REQUIRED, default: false

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required or Optional</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>httpCachingKey</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.requestPath]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A DataWeave expression</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>maxCacheEntries</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10000</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the maximum number of entries that can be stored in the cache at any given time</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>ttl</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>600</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of time (in seconds) after which a single entry expires from the cache</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>useHttpCacheHeaders</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the use of Cache-Control header directives</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>invalidationHeader</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Empty string</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A string specifying the header used to invalidate a single entry or the entire cache</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>requestExpression</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.method == 'GET' or attributes.method == 'HEAD']</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The DataWeave expression that is used to decide which requests are to be cached (caches the response only if the condition is true)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>responseExpression</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[[200, 203, 204, 206, 300, 301, 404, 405, 410, 414, 501] contains attributes.statusCode]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The DataWeave expression that is used to decide which responses are to be cached (caches the response only if the condition is true)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>distributed</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the cache to be distributed among different nodes in a cluster</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>persistCache</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the cache to persist between different restarts of the replica</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Leaving a required value blank configures the parameter as the default value. No value is
> configured for optional values left blank.

#### Resource Configuration Example

```yaml
- policyRef:
    name: http-caching-flex
  config:
    httpCachingKey: "#[attributes.requestPath]"
    maxCacheEntries: 10000
    ttl: 500
    useHttpCacheHeaders: false
    requestExpression: "#[attributes.method == 'GET' or attributes.method == 'HEAD']"
    responseExpression:
      "#[[200, 203, 204, 206, 300, 301, 404, 405, 410, 414, 501] contains attributes.statusCode]"
```

#### Configure Shared Storage for Self-Managed Gateway

Self-Managed Gateways require you to configure a shared storage for distributed caching. For more
information about shared storage, refer to
[Configuring Shared Storage for Omni Gateway in Connected Mode](flex-conn-shared-storage-config).

\--- apiVersion: gateway.mulesoft.com/v1alpha1 kind: Configuration metadata: name:
shared-storage-redis spec: sharedStorage: redis: address: redis:6379 user: user password: pass DB: 1

---

apiVersion: gateway.mulesoft.com/v1alpha1 kind: PolicyBinding metadata: name: ingress-http-caching
spec: targetRef: kind: ApiInstance name: ingress-http policyRef: kind: Extension name:
http-caching-flex config: httpCachingKey: "#\[attributes.method ++ attributes.requestPath\]"
useHttpCacheHeaders: true maxCacheEntries: 100 ttl: 600 distributed: true persistCache: true
invalidationHeader: AGW-CACHE-CONTROL

### Managed Omni Gateway and Omni Gateway Connected Mode

When you apply the policy from the UI, the following parameters are displayed:

Default value: <code>10000</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry Time To Live</p></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time (in seconds) after which a single entry expires from the cache.
